text: The patient is positioned in right lateral decubitus. The surgeon uses trocars to approach the splenic pedicle, mobilizing the left colic angle. The vessels are dissected individually.
The surgeon performs selective dissection of each vessel near the splenic pedicle, carefully isolating them to minimize the risk of hemorrhage.

A visible line of demarcation forms between the ischemic and vascularized sectors of the spleen. The surgeon continues dissecting the splenic pedicle, achieving complete demarcation of the resected area.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to transect the splenic parenchyma in the de-vascularized zone. Hemostasis is achieved with a bipolar clamp, and a hemostatic compress is placed on the sectioned area.
The surgeon extracts the resected splenic tissue, places a drain in the surgical site, and a control scan shows a well-vascularized spleen at four months post-operation.
